title: Unexpected result when using `NULL` constant in comparison
description: The third SELECT statement should return an empty table because the condition `((NULL NOT BETWEEN 1 AND 2) = (t0.c1 NOT IN (t0.c1)))` evaluates to NULL, and no rows should satisfy this condition.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS t1 (c1 INT, c2 VARCHAR, c3 DATE);
INSERT INTO t1(c1, c2, c3) VALUES (1, 'test', DATE '2023-01-01');
INSERT INTO t1(c1, c2, c3) VALUES (2, NULL, DATE '2023-01-02');
SELECT * FROM t1 WHERE (COALESCE(NULL, FALSE) = (t1.c1 NOT IN (SELECT c1 FROM t1 WHERE c2 IS NULL)));
SELECT * FROM t1 WHERE (c2 IS NULL OR COALESCE(NULL, FALSE));
key_bug_features: Comparison involving NULL constant evaluates incorrectly
